apprentice_ash.description: Standard training broom made from ash wood. Steady and forgiving for new flyers.
apprentice_ash.flight_characteristics: Stable, good for beginners, handles light rain

city_birch.description: Urban-adapted broom with reinforced bristles for city flying. Popular with delivery witches.
city_birch.flight_characteristics: Maneuverable in tight spaces, pollution-resistant finish

coastal_driftwood.description: Crafted from sea-weathered wood. Naturally salt-resistant and swift in ocean breezes.
coastal_driftwood.flight_characteristics: Excellent in coastal winds, slightly unpredictable inland

old_hazel.description: Traditional witch's broom. Hazel wood is said to enhance intuition during flight.
old_hazel.flight_characteristics: Slow but wise, excellent for night flying

moonweave.description: Woven with moonlit willow and enchanted with starlight. A prestige broom.
moonweave.flight_characteristics: Silent flight, enhanced night vision for rider

stormchaser.description: Racing broom built for speed and weather resistance. Not for the faint of heart.
stormchaser.flight_characteristics: Extreme speed, handles any weather, requires experienced rider

broom_care.maintenance: Brooms require monthly oiling and bristle grooming
broom_care.storage: Keep in dry place, away from iron
broom_care.lifespan: Well-maintained broom can fly for 20-30 years
broom_care.registration: All flying brooms must be registered with local Witches' Transit Authority

cultural_notes.tradition: A witch's first broom is often a gift at graduation or coming-of-age
cultural_notes.superstition: Never lend your broom during a full moon
cultural_notes.etiquette: Right of way goes to the witch flying uphill
cultural_notes.modern: Electric assist brooms exist but are considered tacky by traditionalists
